mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE increase > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress on earnings recession expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 UNRATE at 9.2% reflects post-crisis labor slack
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20d returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) over 2 months signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 RSAFS at 387.789B shows modest consumer activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with RSAFS declines (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) combined with stable or declining CPI (CPIAUCSL growth < 2% annualized) indicates monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through low discount rates and reduced real rate volatility, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 224.304 reflect post-crisis accommodation
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with contained inflation
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of cases, or the joint signal shows lower predictive power than either component alone (Rank IC difference > 0.01 in favor of single-series model).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 1% annualized over 3 months, signaling slowing economic activity and reduced corporate capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margin compression is anticipated.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 INDPRO at 93.07 reflects post-crisis production recovery plateau
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20d returns in fewer than 52% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline > 10% month-over-month signals deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking weakness in construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as GDP growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 HOUST at 560.0K reflects post-crisis housing weakness
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with housing sector vulnerability
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60d returns in fewer than 54% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50K new jobs per month over 3 months while UNRATE remains elevated above 8.5%, indicating labor market stagnation that constrains consumer income growth and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 PAYEMS at 131.017M and UNRATE at 9.2% reflect weak labor recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5d returns in fewer than 53% of cases, or the joint signal shows lower predictive power than UNRATE alone (Rank IC difference > 0.005).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized over the most recent quarter while retail sales (RSAFS) show month-over-month contraction, signaling an economy approaching stall-speed that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2011 GDPC1 at 13.444T and June RSAFS at 387.789B reflect slowing demand
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20d returns in fewer than 56% of cases, or the interaction term adds less than 0.01 to Rank IC compared to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% annualized while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.5%, creating a negative real rate regime that signals future monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity duration risk increases.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 CPIAUCSL at 224.304 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% reflect accommodative stance amid inflation concerns
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60d returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the joint signal shows lower predictive power than either component alone (Rank IC difference > 0.015).
